docstring.text: Functionality to normalize messages into compatible formats for targets.

Normalizer that converts a list of Messages to a list of ChatMessages.

This normalizer handles both single-part and multipart messages:
- Single piece messages: content is a simple string
- Multiple piece messages: content is a list of dicts with type/text or type/image_url

params.name: use_developer_role
params.desc:
If True, translates "system" role to "developer" role
for compatibility with newer OpenAI models (o1, o3, gpt-4.1+).
Defaults to False for backward compatibility.

params.name: system_message_behavior
params.desc:
How to handle system messages before conversion.
- "keep": Keep system messages as-is (default)
- "squash": Merge system message into first user message
- "ignore": Drop system messages entirely

docstring.text:
Convert a list of Messages to a list of ChatMessages.

For single-piece text messages, content is a string.
For multi-piece or non-text messages, content is a list of content dicts.

params.name: messages
params.desc: The list of Message objects to normalize.

raises.desc: If the messages list is empty.

docstring.text:
Convert a list of Messages to a JSON string representation.

This serializes the list of ChatMessages to JSON format.

params.name: messages
params.desc: The list of Message objects to normalize.

returns.desc: A JSON string representation of the ChatMessages.

params.name: use_developer_role
params.desc: If True, translates "system" role to "developer" role.

params.name: system_message_behavior
params.desc: How to handle system messages. Defaults to "keep".

docstring.text:
Normalizer that formats conversation history as turn-based text.

This is the standard format used by attacks like Crescendo and TAP
for including conversation context in adversarial chat prompts.
The output format is:

    Turn 1:
    User: <content>
    Assistant: <content>

    Turn 2:
    User: <content>
    ...
Normalize a list of messages into a turn-based context string.

params.name: messages
params.desc: The list of Message objects to normalize.

returns.desc: A formatted string with turn numbers and role prefixes.

raises.desc: If the messages list is empty.

docstring.text: Normalizer that combines the first system message with the first user message using generic instruction tags.

params.name: messages
params.desc: The list of messages to normalize.

returns.desc: A Message with the system message squashed into the first user message.

raises.desc: If the messages list is empty.

docstring.text:
Abstract base class for normalizers that return a list of items.

Subclasses specify the type T (e.g., Message, ChatMessage) that the list contains.
T must implement the DictConvertible protocol (have a to_dict() method).
Normalize the list of messages into a list of items.

params.name: messages
params.desc: The list of Message objects to normalize.

returns.desc: A list of normalized items of type T.

docstring.text:
Normalize the list of messages into a list of dictionaries.

This method uses normalize_async and calls to_dict() on each item.

params.name: messages
params.desc: The list of Message objects to normalize.

returns.desc: A list of dictionaries representing the normalized messages.

docstring.text:
Abstract base class for normalizers that return a string representation.

Use this for formatting messages into text for non-chat targets or context strings.
Normalize the list of messages into a string representation.

params.name: messages
params.desc: The list of Message objects to normalize.

returns.desc: A string representation of the messages.

docstring.text:
Enable application of the chat template stored in a Hugging Face tokenizer
to a list of messages. For more details, see
https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/main/en/chat_templating.

Create a normalizer from a model name or alias.

This factory method simplifies creating a normalizer by handling tokenizer
loading automatically. Use aliases for common models or provide a full
HuggingFace model path.

params.name: model_name_or_alias
params.desc:
Either a full HuggingFace model name or an alias
(e.g., 'chatml', 'phi3', 'llama3'). See MODEL_ALIASES for available aliases.

params.name: token
params.desc:
Optional HuggingFace token for gated models. If not provided,
falls back to HUGGINGFACE_TOKEN environment variable.

params.name: system_message_behavior
params.desc:
Override how to handle system messages.
If not provided, uses the model's default config.

returns.desc: TokenizerTemplateNormalizer configured with the model's tokenizer.

raises.desc: If the tokenizer doesn't have a chat_template.

docstring.text:
Apply the chat template stored in the tokenizer to a list of messages.

Handles system messages based on the configured system_message_behavior:
- "keep": Pass system messages as-is
- "squash": Merge system into first user message
- "ignore": Drop system messages entirely
- "developer": Change system role to developer role

returns.desc: The formatted chat messages as a string.

docstring.text: Initialize an instance of the TokenizerTemplateNormalizer class.

params.name: tokenizer
params.desc: A Hugging Face tokenizer with a chat template.

params.name: system_message_behavior
params.desc:
How to handle system messages. Options:
- "keep": Keep system messages as-is (default)
- "squash": Merge system into first user message
- "ignore": Drop system messages entirely
- "developer": Change system role to developer role
